name: Lighter
description: Disposable and Zippo lighters are allowed in carry-on baggage but generally prohibited in checked bags. Only one lighter per passenger is permitted and it must remain on your person (not in carry-on bag).
tsaGuideline: One common lighter (disposable BIC-type or Zippo) is permitted per passenger, carried on your person or in carry-on. Torch lighters and arc/plasma lighters are prohibited in both carry-on and checked bags. Lighters may not be placed in checked baggage unless they are DOT-approved lighter cases.
iataGuideline: IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations allow one small lighter on the person (not in carry-on bag or checked bag) that uses a flammable gas or flammable liquid fuel. Lighter fuel/refills are not permitted.
restrictions: Must be kept on your person (pocket), not in carry-on bag
Torch lighters (jet flame/blue flame) are prohibited everywhere
Not allowed in checked bags unless in DOT-approved case without fuel
Lighter fluid and refills are prohibited in both carry-on and checked bags

faqs.question: Can I pack a lighter in my checked luggage?
faqs.answer: No. Lighters are prohibited in checked baggage unless they are completely empty of fuel or stored in a DOT-approved protective case. A DOT-approved case must be able to contain a fire if the lighter accidentally ignites.

faqs.question: Are Zippo lighters allowed on planes?
faqs.answer: Yes. One Zippo lighter is allowed per passenger, but it must be carried on your person (in a pocket). You cannot pack it in your carry-on bag or checked bag. Zippo lighter fluid refills are not permitted.

faqs.question: What about torch lighters or electric arc lighters?
faqs.answer: Torch lighters (also called jet lighters or blue flame lighters) are completely prohibited in carry-on, checked bags, and on your person. Standard electric arc/plasma lighters follow the same one-lighter rule but policies vary by airline.
